task_language: pick up the cup and place it in the back compartment of the caddy
strategy_notes: WINNING RECIPE replicated from the solved seed-0 reference at a single pi0_pick call with the FULL task_language as prompt, lift_thresh=0.5 (so the early-exit lift heuristic never fires) plus gripper_closed_thresh=0.0 — per memory wisdom, this turns pi0_pick into a generic closed-loop CONTACT driver and lets Pi0 run its trained pick+place trajectory to completion. Pi0 grasped the white_yellow_mug (the only cup-shaped object in the scene; black_book_1 is the only distractor, a flat book), carried it to the desk_caddy back compartment, and the predicate fired. SUCCESS first try on this run: chunks_used=32/50, peak_lift=0.3162m, descent=0.1987m, final_gripper_opening=0.0784, libero_terminated=true. Perception pass identified: white_yellow_mug in foreground (clearly the cup, ceramic with yellow interior), desk_caddy at back-left of workspace (brown wooden multi-compartment organizer), black_book on right of mug (flat distractor). Reference s0 strategy_notes documented the prior failures that led to this recipe: manual carry + pi0_doubled both miss the cavity because Pi0's intended placement needs Pi0's full trained trajectory; using pi0_pick-as-closed-loop with verbatim task_language is the cleanest path for caddy placement.
